description: You are conducting a wetland delineation field verification survey for a proposed development near La Crosse, Wisconsin. Open wetland_delineation_verification.gpkg in QField. The project contains three layers: wetland_boundaries (6 polygons needing verification), soil_borings (14 boring locations with field indicator data), and verification_results (empty, for your findings). For each wetland, examine its associated soil borings matched by the wetland_id attribute. A wetland is CONFIRMED if at least 2 of its borings have is_wetland_positive = 1; otherwise it is REJECTED. For all 6 wetlands, update field_verified to CONFIRMED or REJECTED, set verification_date to 2024-09-15, and set delineator to Survey Team Alpha. For REJECTED wetlands only, also set boundary_accuracy to FAILED. For each REJECTED wetland, create a point in verification_results near its center with result_id set to the wetland_id followed by _RESULT, wetland_id set to the wetland's ID, finding set to INSUFFICIENT_HYDRIC_INDICATORS, and notes listing the negative boring IDs. Finally, among CONFIRMED wetlands, find the one with the shallowest average depth_to_water_cm across its borings and update that wetland's nwi_status to primary_reference. Save all changes.

metadata.occupation_name: Environmental Science and Protection Technicians, Including Health
metadata.data_source: USACE wetland delineation protocols, Upper Mississippi River floodplain, La Crosse WI
